# Prompt Technique Exploration and Comparison

## Project Overview

This project explores and compares five major prompt engineering techniques used with Large Language Models (LLMs):

1. Zero-Shot Prompting
2. Few-Shot Prompting
3. Role Prompting
4. Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Prompting
5. Tree-of-Thought (ToT) Prompting

The objective is to evaluate how different prompting strategies influence the quality, accuracy, reasoning capability, and style of LLM-generated responses.

## Objectives

* Study various prompting techniques.
* Compare outputs generated by different prompts.
* Analyze the impact of examples and context.
* Evaluate reasoning capabilities of LLMs.
* Build a reusable prompt engineering reference.

## Technologies Used

* Python
* Google Colab
* OpenAI API
* Pandas
* Matplotlib
* Python-Docx

## Project Structure

Project/
├── README.md
├── Prompt_Collection.docx
├── Output_Comparison_Report.docx
├── Prompt_Engineering.ipynb
├── prompt_outputs.csv
├── prompts/
└── report/

## Expected Outcomes

* Understanding of prompt engineering techniques.
* Performance comparison among prompting methods.
* Structured prompt library for future applications.
* Analysis of context and persona influence.
